phase.name: Pre-flight validation
phase.description: Verify ALL prerequisites locally before spending any RunPod credits. Every check is programmatic — NEVER ask the user about infrastructure state.

task.name: Validate .env has all required RunPod dev vars
task.description: Check that .env contains non-empty values for every variable that dev_runpod.yaml references. Missing or empty vars cause silent failures on the pod (metalearning ML-8: skip amnesia).

tdd.red: Write test that reads .env and asserts these vars are non-empty: RUNPOD_API_KEY, DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL, DVC_S3_ACCESS_KEY, DVC_S3_SECRET_KEY, DVC_S3_BUCKET, MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI, MLFLOW_CLOUD_USERNAME, MLFLOW_CLOUD_PASSWORD, HF_TOKEN. Test file: tests/v2/unit/test_runpod_dev_preflight.py
tdd.green: Script scripts/validate_runpod_dev_env.py that reads .env, checks each required var, prints clear error for missing ones, exits non-zero if any missing.

task.acceptance: All 9 env vars present and non-empty in .env. Script exits 0. No "ask the user" — just check.

task.name: Verify SkyPilot RunPod backend enabled + version check
task.description: Run 'sky check runpod' programmatically. Also check SkyPilot version (task.update_envs() API requires 0.6+). If it fails, print exact fix command.

tdd.red: Test that calls subprocess.run(['sky', 'check', 'runpod']) and asserts returncode == 0. Also check importlib.metadata.version("skypilot") >= "0.6.0".
tdd.green: Add to validate_runpod_dev_env.py: sky check + version check.

task.acceptance: sky check runpod exits 0. SkyPilot >= 0.6.0.

task.name: Verify UpCloud MLflow is reachable
task.description: HTTP GET to MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI/health. Must return 200. Include basic auth credentials from .env. Metalearning ML-4: check programmatically, don't ask.

tdd.red: Test that reads MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI from .env, sends HTTP GET with basic auth, asserts status 200. Auto-skips if MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI not set (but logs WARNING, not silent skip).
tdd.green: Add to validate_runpod_dev_env.py: urllib.request with basic auth.

task.acceptance: MLflow health endpoint returns HTTP 200.

task.name: Verify DVC data synced to UpCloud S3
task.description: Run 'dvc status -r upcloud' and check output. Data must already be pushed (350 files, ~2.6 GB).

tdd.red: Test that runs dvc status -r upcloud and asserts no "new" or "modified" entries (all in sync).
tdd.green: Add to validate_runpod_dev_env.py: subprocess dvc status check. If not synced, print: "Run 'dvc push -r upcloud' first."

task.acceptance: DVC remote status shows "Data and calculation will not change" or empty diff.

task.name: Verify .skyignore sync size + critical file inclusion
task.description:
Metalearning ML-1: unanchored globs caused 12 GB syncs. Measure actual sync size. Must be under 100 MB. Current measurement: 2955 files, 36.5 MB.
REVIEWER FIX: Also verify critical files are NOT excluded: - configs/splits/smoke_test_1fold_4vol.json (needed by setup block) - configs/experiment/smoke_*.yaml (needed by run block) - src/minivess/ (the actual code) Print top-10 largest synced files for surprise detection.

tdd.red: Test that walks the project directory respecting .skyignore patterns and asserts: 1. Total size under 100 MB 2. configs/splits/smoke_test_1fold_4vol.json IS included 3. configs/experiment/smoke_dynunet.yaml IS included
tdd.green: Add to validate_runpod_dev_env.py: os.walk with exclusion logic, sum file sizes, check critical file inclusion, print top-10 largest.

task.acceptance: Sync size under 100 MB. Critical files included. Print actual size + top-10 for operator awareness.

task.name: Harden MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env var resolution
task.description:
KNOWN BUG (issue #689): dev_runpod.yaml has MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: ${MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI} in envs section. SkyPilot may pass this literally (not resolved) OR as empty string.
launch_dev_runpod.py ALREADY handles this correctly (lines 109-124): it reads MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI from .env and sets MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI directly via task.update_envs(). The YAML envs section is a fallback.
REVIEWER FIX (C2): Guard must catch THREE failure modes: 1. Literal "${MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI}" string (SkyPilot didn't resolve) 2. Empty string (MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI was empty in .env) 3. "mlruns" fallback (resolve_tracking_uri() silent default) All three result in artifacts going to local disk and being lost.
REVIEWER FIX (S2): Also test MLflow connectivity FROM the pod, not just from local machine. Pod may have different network path.

tdd.red: Test that parses dev_runpod.yaml run: block and asserts it contains guards for all three MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI failure modes.
tdd.green: Add to dev_runpod.yaml run block (before training execution): ``` echo "MLflow URI resolved: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}" echo "MLflow auth: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME}" # Guard: catch literal ${}, empty, or local-only URI if [ -z "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}" ] || \ [ "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}" = "mlruns" ] || \ echo "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}" | grep -q '[$]{'; then echo "FATAL: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI is empty, local-only, or unresolved." echo "Value: '${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}'" echo "Use launch_dev_runpod.py, not 'sky launch' directly." exit 1 fi # Guard: MLflow auth must be set if [ -z "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME}" ] || [ -z "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD}" ]; then echo "FATAL: MLflow auth credentials missing." exit 1 fi # Connectivity check from pod (reviewer S2) if ! curl -sf -u "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME}:${MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD}" \ "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}/health" > /dev/null 2>&1; then echo "WARNING: Cannot reach MLflow from pod: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}" echo "Training will proceed but artifacts may not upload." fi ```

task.acceptance: Run block contains guards for all three failure modes. MLflow connectivity tested from pod before training starts.

task.name: Fix python symlink in setup block
task.description:
REVIEWER FIX (C1): The setup block uses bare `python` for weight downloads and verification after creating a Python 3.13 venv. If the venv only creates `python3.13` symlink (not `python`), bare `python` resolves to system Python 3.10/3.11 which lacks project deps. Weight downloads fail silently (|| true), and verification checks crash on import.
Fix: Use explicit ${WORKDIR}/.venv/bin/python for all calls, or add symlink verification after venv creation.

tdd.red: Test that parses dev_runpod.yaml setup: block and asserts no bare `python -c` calls exist after venv creation — all should use explicit venv path or ${WORKDIR}/.venv/bin/python.
tdd.green: In dev_runpod.yaml setup block, after venv creation add: ``` # Verify python symlink exists in venv (reviewer C1 fix) ls -la ${WORKDIR}/.venv/bin/python* | head -5 ``` Replace all `python -c` calls with `${WORKDIR}/.venv/bin/python -c` or simply ensure `python` is on PATH via the export.

task.acceptance: All python calls in setup use explicit venv python. `python --version` in setup shows 3.13, not system Python.

phase.name: DynUNet plumbing test
phase.description:
Cheapest possible E2E test: DynUNet (3.5 GB VRAM) on RunPod RTX 4090. This verifies the plumbing (DVC pull, training, MLflow upload) works before risking credits on heavy models.
DynUNet can train locally on 8 GB GPU, but this test validates the CLOUD PIPELINE, not the model.
Cost: RTX 4090 $0.44/hr × ~20 min (setup + training) = ~$0.15

task.name: Launch DynUNet on RunPod + teardown after
task.description:
Run: uv run python scripts/launch_dev_runpod.py --model dynunet (or: make dev-gpu MODEL=dynunet)
Monitor via: sky logs minivess-dev
REVIEWER FIX (H5): After training completes and verification passes, tear down the cluster: sky down minivess-dev -y This prevents idle billing between phases.
Expected: - Setup phase: ~8-12 min (Python install, uv sync, DVC pull) - Training phase: ~2-3 min (2 epochs, 1 fold, 4 volumes) - Total: ~15-20 min - Cost: ~$0.15 (RTX 4090 $0.44/hr × 0.33 hr)
task.acceptance: sky logs shows "Training complete" or train_flow.py exit 0. Pod status: SUCCEEDED (not FAILED, not CANCELLED).

task.name: Verify MLflow artifacts on UpCloud (with size + sanity checks)
task.description:
Query UpCloud MLflow server for the DynUNet training run.
REVIEWER FIX (I1): dev_runpod.yaml creates experiments named "dev_{uuid}_{model}" (line 216), NOT "smoke_test_{model}". The verification script must search for "dev_" prefix, not "smoke_test_".
Verify: 1. Run exists with status FINISHED 2. Params logged (model_family=dynunet, loss_name, max_epochs) 3. Metrics logged: train_loss present AND 0.01 < train_loss < 5.0 (REVIEWER FIX: sanity range, not just "exists") 4. Artifacts present AND total size > 1 MB for DynUNet (REVIEWER FIX: check size, not just count. DynUNet checkpoint ~4 MB; 0-byte = truncated upload) 5. Hydra config artifact logged (issue #674)
CRITICAL: If params/metrics logged but NO artifacts, that's the silent upload failure from commit da4d93a. This must be caught, not ignored.

tdd.red: Test that queries UpCloud MLflow API for the most recent experiment matching "dev_*_dynunet", asserts: - run.info.status == "FINISHED" - "model_family" in run.data.params - train_loss in run.data.metrics, 0.01 < value < 5.0 - artifacts exist with total size > 1 MB
tdd.green: Update scripts/verify_smoke_test.py to: 1. Search for both "dev_" and "smoke_test_" experiment prefixes 2. Add artifact size check via client.list_artifacts() 3. Add train_loss sanity range check If artifacts empty or undersized: "FAIL: Artifacts not uploaded. Check MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI resolution on the pod."

task.acceptance: Run FINISHED + params + metrics (sensible values) + artifacts (> 1 MB). If artifacts missing or zero-size: FAIL (do not proceed to Phase 2).

task.name: Teardown Phase 1 pod
task.description:
REVIEWER FIX (H5, H4): Tear down cluster between phases to: 1. Prevent idle billing 2. Force fresh setup for Phase 2 (avoids state leakage) 3. Prevent cluster name collision with next launch
Run: sky down minivess-dev -y Verify: sky status shows no minivess-dev cluster.
task.acceptance: No minivess-dev cluster in sky status output.

phase.name: sam3_hybrid verification
phase.description:
First heavy model that CANNOT train on the local 8 GB GPU. sam3_hybrid: 7.18 GiB measured at peak on RTX 2070 Super (64x64x3 patch), which OOMed on the 8 GB card. Needs ≥16 GB recommended. On RTX 4090 (24 GB): plenty of headroom.
VRAM source: configs/model_profiles/sam3_hybrid.yaml (metalearning ML-9: never estimate, always read the file)
REVIEWER FIX (I4): sam3_hybrid 7.18 GiB was the peak-at-OOM on 8 GB card, not a successful training measurement. Actual training VRAM on RTX 4090 will be measured here for the first time.
phase.depends-on: P1 (T1.2 + T1.3 green — plumbing verified, pod torn down)

task.name: Launch sam3_hybrid on RunPod
task.description:
Run: uv run python scripts/launch_dev_runpod.py --model sam3_hybrid (or: make dev-gpu MODEL=sam3_hybrid)
Uses smoke_sam3_hybrid experiment config: - 2 epochs, 1 fold, 4 volumes - val_interval=3 (> max_epochs=2, so validation skipped — OOM prevention) - pretrained: false (smoke test validates pipeline, not pretrained quality)
Expected: - Setup: ~8-12 min (fresh pod, full install + DVC pull) - Training: ~5-8 min - Cost: ~$0.15-0.20 (RTX 4090 $0.44/hr × ~0.4 hr)
task.acceptance: Training completes without OOM. Pod status: SUCCEEDED.

task.name: Verify sam3_hybrid artifacts on UpCloud MLflow
task.description:
Same verification as T1.2 but for sam3_hybrid: 1. Run FINISHED on UpCloud MLflow 2. model_family=sam3_hybrid in params 3. train_loss metric: present AND 0.01 < value < 5.0 4. Checkpoint artifact uploaded AND size > 1 MB (sam3_hybrid checkpoint ~2.5 GB — if size < 100 MB, truncated)
Record actual VRAM usage from pod logs: - Run `nvidia-smi` after training in run block - Extract prof_cuda_peak_allocated_mb from MLflow metrics if profiling enabled - Or check sky logs for nvidia-smi output during setup verification
REVIEWER FIX: Add post-training nvidia-smi to run block.
task.acceptance: Run FINISHED + params + metrics + artifacts (> 1 MB) on UpCloud MLflow. Measured VRAM recorded in issue #688 comment.

task.name: Teardown Phase 2 pod
task.description: Tear down cluster for fresh Phase 3 start. Run: sky down minivess-dev -y
task.acceptance: No minivess-dev cluster in sky status.

phase.name: VesselFM verification
phase.description:
Second heavy model that CANNOT train on the local 8 GB GPU. VesselFM needs ~10 GB VRAM (ESTIMATED, not yet measured). On RTX 4090 (24 GB): should fit with margin.
VRAM source: configs/model_profiles/vesselfm.yaml Note: This phase will provide the FIRST real measurement.
REVIEWER FIX: VesselFM OOM fallback documented in error recovery section.
phase.depends-on: P2 (T2.2 + T2.3 green — sam3_hybrid works, pod torn down)

task.name: Launch VesselFM on RunPod
task.description:
Run: uv run python scripts/launch_dev_runpod.py --model vesselfm (or: make dev-gpu MODEL=vesselfm)
Uses smoke_vesselfm experiment config: - 2 epochs, 1 fold, 4 volumes - HF_TOKEN required for weight download (pre-cached in setup)
REVIEWER FIX: If VesselFM OOMs on RTX 4090 (unlikely but possible since VRAM is ESTIMATED at ~10 GB), fallback options: a) Reduce patch_size in smoke_vesselfm.yaml b) Ensure AMP/FP16 is enabled c) Try A40 (48 GB, $0.39/hr on RunPod) — needs accelerators list update d) File issue documenting actual VRAM and skip this phase
Expected: - Setup: ~10-15 min (VesselFM weights download from HF) - Training: ~8-10 min - Cost: ~$0.20-0.25 (RTX 4090 $0.44/hr × ~0.5 hr)
task.acceptance: Training completes without OOM. Pod status: SUCCEEDED.

task.name: Verify VesselFM artifacts on UpCloud MLflow
task.description:
Same verification as T2.2 but for VesselFM: 1. Run FINISHED on UpCloud MLflow 2. model_family=vesselfm in params 3. train_loss metric: present AND 0.01 < value < 5.0 4. Checkpoint artifact uploaded AND size > 1 MB (VesselFM checkpoint ~120 MB)
Record ACTUAL VRAM usage — this will be the FIRST measurement.
task.acceptance: Run FINISHED + params + metrics + artifacts on UpCloud MLflow. VesselFM VRAM measured and recorded.

task.name: Final pod teardown
task.description: Tear down cluster after all verification complete. Run: sky down minivess-dev -y Verify no RunPod pods remain running.
task.acceptance: No minivess-dev cluster. Zero ongoing RunPod billing.

phase.name: Documentation and closure
phase.description: Record results, update documentation, close issues, verify costs. This phase makes the work permanent and discoverable.
phase.depends-on: P3 (T3.3 green — all models verified, pods down)

task.name: Update issue #688 with verified results + actual costs
task.description:
Post comment on #688 with: - DynUNet: VRAM, duration, cost, MLflow run ID - sam3_hybrid: VRAM, duration, cost, MLflow run ID - VesselFM: VRAM, duration, cost, MLflow run ID - Total ACTUAL cost vs budget ($2.00) - Close issue as verified.
REVIEWER FIX: Include actual costs from RunPod billing dashboard. Compare against budget. Record any variance.
task.acceptance: Issue #688 closed with verification evidence + cost audit.

task.name: Close related issues with evidence
task.description: Close or update these issues based on verification results: - #689: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI resolution → close if artifacts appeared - #668: VesselFM training launch → close if Phase 3 succeeded - #671: VesselFM smoke test params → close if params verified - #674: Hydra config artifact → close if config artifact in MLflow - #675: sys_bench params → close if bench metrics logged If NOT working, update each with diagnostics from pod logs.
task.acceptance: Related issues closed or updated with evidence.

task.name: Update model profiles with measured VRAM
task.description:
Update configs/model_profiles/sam3_hybrid.yaml and configs/model_profiles/vesselfm.yaml with measured values from RTX 4090 training runs. Metalearning ML-9: measured > estimated, always.
Add fields to notes section: rtx4090_training_vram_gb: X.X rtx4090_measured_date: "2026-03-14" rtx4090_patch_size: [64, 64, 3]
task.acceptance: Both model profiles have RTX 4090 training VRAM measurements.

task.name: Document RunPod dev workflow
task.description: Add clear section to deployment/CLAUDE.md or similar explaining: - When to use RunPod (dev, no Docker, quick prototyping, >8 GB VRAM models) - When to use Lambda Labs (staging/prod, Docker mandatory) - make dev-gpu MODEL=X as the primary command - make dev-gpu-heavy for sam3_hybrid + vesselfm - EXPERIMENT env var auto-maps to configs/experiment/smoke_{model}.yaml - Prerequisites: .env with all cloud vars populated - Cost estimates per model (measured, not estimated)
task.acceptance: Documentation exists explaining the three-environment model with concrete commands and measured cost/VRAM data.

task.name: Update MEMORY.md with verification results
task.description: REVIEWER FIX (CLAUDE.md Rule #14): Persist all insights to durable files. Update MEMORY.md with: - RunPod verification complete date - Measured VRAM for sam3_hybrid and VesselFM on RTX 4090 - Actual cost of verification - Any surprises or lessons learned
task.acceptance: MEMORY.md updated with verification results.

anti-patterns.check: Verified: .skyignore uses /data/ not data/. Sync size: 36.5 MB.
dev_runpod.yaml has NO image_id field. Correct for container-based RunPod.
N/A for dev_runpod.yaml (uses workdir rsync, not Docker image_id). SkyPilot SSHes as root into RunPod pod regardless.
All preflight checks in T0.1-T0.5 are programmatic. validate_runpod_dev_env.py checks everything.
RunPod dev is the APPROVED non-Docker escape hatch (CLAUDE.md). MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 + PREFECT_DISABLED=1 are explicitly permitted.
SkyPilot = intercloud broker (compute placement), not IaC. SkyPilot decides cheapest GPU from {RTX4090, RTX5090, RTX3090}.

check.name: Bare VM setup is approved for dev

anti-patterns.check: dev_runpod.yaml uses uv sync in setup block — APPROVED for dev tier. Staging/prod must use Docker image_id (Lambda Labs).
T0.1 treats missing env vars as FATAL, not skip. T1.2 treats empty artifacts as FATAL, not "expected". REVIEWER FIX: T1.2 also checks artifact SIZE, not just existence.

sam3_hybrid: configs/model_profiles/sam3_hybrid.yaml VesselFM: configs/model_profiles/vesselfm.yaml No estimates from architecture descriptions. REVIEWER FIX: sam3_hybrid 7.18 GiB is OOM-at-peak, not training success.
Each phase tears down minivess-dev before next phase launches. Prevents state leakage, stale setup, and naming conflicts.

check.name: No bare python calls in venv context (REVIEWER NEW)

anti-patterns.check: All python -c calls in setup use ${WORKDIR}/.venv/bin/python or are after explicit PATH export verified by symlink check.

scenario.name: sky launch fails immediately
scenario.diagnostic: 1. Check sky check runpod output — API key valid? 2. Check GPU availability: sky show-gpus --cloud runpod 3. If all GPUs sold out: wait and retry, or fall back to Lambda Labs

scenario.name: Setup fails (non-zero exit)
scenario.diagnostic: 1. sky logs minivess-dev --setup — find specific error line 2. If Python install fails: deadsnakes PPA may be down, use default Python 3. If uv sync fails: missing system library. SSH in (make dev-gpu-ssh), run uv sync --all-extras manually, note missing apt packages 4. If DVC pull fails: check S3 credentials, UpCloud endpoint reachability SSH in, run dvc pull -r upcloud -v for verbose output

scenario.name: Training fails but setup succeeded
scenario.diagnostic: 1. sky logs minivess-dev — find Python traceback 2. If CUDA OOM: check model profile VRAM vs actual GPU. Reduce patch_size. 3. If MLflow connection error: check MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI diagnostic output 4. If import error: uv sync missed a dep. SSH in, install manually. 5. If HF weight download error: check HF_TOKEN, try manual download

scenario.name: Training succeeds but MLflow empty on UpCloud
scenario.diagnostic: 1. Check pod logs for MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI diagnostic echo 2. If URI was local "mlruns": launcher didn't set env var correctly 3. If URI was correct but artifacts empty: connection dropped during upload → increase MLFLOW_ARTIFACT_UPLOAD_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT → try: SSH to pod, manually upload checkpoint via mlflow CLI 4. If params/metrics present but artifacts empty: try/except in upload code swallowed the error (commit da4d93a) → fix the swallowing

scenario.name: VesselFM OOM on RTX 4090
scenario.diagnostic: 1. Check nvidia-smi output for peak VRAM 2. ESTIMATED 10 GB may be wrong — first real measurement 3. Fallback options (in order): a. Reduce patch_size in smoke_vesselfm.yaml (smallest that works) b. Enable gradient checkpointing if supported c. Try A40 (48 GB, $0.39/hr) — add to accelerators temporarily d. File issue with measured VRAM, skip this phase, revisit later

scenario.name: Pod keeps running after training (idle billing)
scenario.diagnostic: 1. Check idle_minutes_to_autostop setting (should be 15) 2. Manual stop: sky down minivess-dev -y 3. Verify: sky status — no clusters listed 4. If sky down fails: use RunPod console to terminate pod directly

risk.name: MLflow artifact upload fails silently
risk.description: Commit da4d93a wrapped uploads in try/except. Large .pth files may fail to upload to UpCloud MLflow (connection drops).
risk.mitigation: T1.2 checks artifact SIZE, not just existence (reviewer fix). If empty or undersized: do NOT proceed, fix upload first. Potential fix: increase MLFLOW_ARTIFACT_UPLOAD_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT. Fallback: SSH to pod, manually upload via mlflow CLI.

risk.name: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI resolves to empty or local
risk.description: REVIEWER FIX (C2): Three failure modes for the URI: 1. Literal "${MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI}" (SkyPilot didn't resolve) 2. Empty string (MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI empty in .env) 3. "mlruns" (resolve_tracking_uri() silent fallback) All three cause artifacts to go to local disk and be lost.
risk.mitigation: T0.6 adds guards for all three modes in the run block. Training will ABORT if URI is empty, local, or unresolved.

risk.name: RunPod RTX 4090/5090/3090 all sold out
risk.description: RunPod community GPUs have limited availability. All three consumer GPU types might be unavailable.
risk.mitigation: Budget allows retries. SkyPilot queues and retries automatically. If consistently unavailable: fall back to Lambda Labs A10 for verification.

risk.name: uv sync fails on RunPod base image
risk.description: RunPod's pre-cached image may lack system libraries for compiled Python packages (h5py, lxml, psycopg2, etc.). REVIEWER FIX: SkyPilot injects its own runtime (SSH, ray) which may conflict with Python 3.13 + project deps.
risk.mitigation: dev_runpod.yaml installs Python 3.13 from deadsnakes PPA. If fails: SSH in, check specific error, install missing apt packages. Last resort: switch to RunPod PyTorch template (has more system deps).

risk.name: Cluster name collision between phases
risk.description: REVIEWER FIX (H4): All phases use cluster name "minivess-dev". If Phase 1 pod is still running when Phase 2 launches, SkyPilot may reuse the pod without re-running setup.
risk.mitigation: Explicit sky down between phases (T1.3, T2.3, T3.3). Each phase starts fresh with full setup.

risk.name: VesselFM HF weight download timeout
risk.description: VesselFM weights (~2 GB) downloaded during setup via HuggingFace. RunPod pod network may be slow.
risk.mitigation: Setup block pre-caches weights with || true (non-fatal). If download fails: training will re-attempt in train_flow.py. HF_TOKEN is in .env and passed to pod.

cost-summary.total: ~$0.60 happy path, ~$1.50 with retries, $2.00 hard cap
